jokes.text: You want me to fan you? I don't think so, Louie. You see, I left my palm leaf back at the hut this morning.
jokes.setup: Louie asks Bobby to fan him
jokes.punchline: Bobby sarcastically references colonial/racist stereotypes about servants
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that establishes character dynamic - Bobby won't be Louie's servant, delivered with wit rather than anger

jokes.text: Go ahead. Make it racial right away.
jokes.setup: Bobby makes a joke about colonial servitude
jokes.punchline: Louie calls out the racial undertones while missing his own insensitivity
jokes.explanation: Louie's obliviousness to his own behavior is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Now, you know that I'm not the kind of mother who comes in with boring stories about her kids, right? [Everyone enthusiastically agrees]
jokes.setup: Elaine claims she doesn't bore people with kid stories
jokes.punchline: Everyone eagerly agrees, revealing she absolutely does this constantly
jokes.explanation: Classic setup where character lacks self-awareness, and the group's overeager agreement sells it perfectly

jokes.text: Oh, I forgot. We're only allowed to talk about hangovers and the Jets.
jokes.setup: The guys dismiss her potential kid story
jokes.punchline: She points out their conversational limitations
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about male conversational topics

jokes.text: Hey, did you see the Jet game this weekend? No. I was hung over.
jokes.setup: Elaine just mocked them for only talking about hangovers and Jets
jokes.punchline: Someone immediately proves her point perfectly
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback that validates Elaine's observation with impeccable timing

jokes.text: You know, when I was five and a half and I was just so high and I was so cute, you could eat me up alive... Wait, wait, wait. You're telling me a kid story about yourself? Yes, because you know, I am a bachelor. I am the only kid I've got.
jokes.setup: Latka starts telling a cute kid story about himself
jokes.punchline: His logic: as a bachelor, he's the only kid he has
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Latka logic - absurd but internally consistent with his innocent worldview

jokes.text: Bobby, I'd rather you wouldn't. Why not? Well, I mean, if you had a sister you wouldn't want a guy like you to meet her, would ya?
jokes.setup: Bobby wants to meet Tony's sister
jokes.punchline: Tony tells Bobby he wouldn't want someone like Bobby meeting his own sister
jokes.explanation: Tony's protective brother instincts delivered with brutal honesty about Bobby's character

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, I'll hang around the back till she leaves.
jokes.setup: Tony insults Bobby about not being good enough for his sister
jokes.punchline: Bobby immediately accepts this assessment without protest
jokes.explanation: Bobby's complete lack of self-defense reveals his character's low self-esteem

jokes.text: I doubt she's even a virgin anymore.
jokes.setup: Tony tries to reassure Alex about dating his sister
jokes.punchline: Tony's idea of reassurance involves discussing his sister's virginity
jokes.explanation: Tony's well-meaning but inappropriate oversharing creates perfect cringe comedy

jokes.text: I want you to know I thought about it and it's okay with me if you want to go to bed with Monica.
jokes.setup: Tony continues trying to set up Alex with his sister
jokes.punchline: Tony gives explicit permission for Alex to sleep with Monica
jokes.explanation: Tony's complete lack of boundaries escalates the discomfort perfectly

jokes.text: I better warn you, this might lead to dinner.
jokes.setup: Tony warns Alex about sleeping with Monica
jokes.punchline: The 'warning' is that it might lead to dinner
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of expectations - warning about escalation to something completely innocent

jokes.text: I'm beginning to get a crush on myself here.
jokes.setup: Tony keeps praising Alex to convince Monica to date him
jokes.punchline: Alex acknowledges Tony's sales pitch is working on him too
jokes.explanation: Alex's dry self-awareness about Tony's overselling creates perfect meta-humor

jokes.text: Listen, who are you fooling? I would marry the wench.
jokes.setup: Latka asks if he can date Tony's sister too
jokes.punchline: Latka's commitment level goes straight to marriage, calling her 'wench'
jokes.explanation: Latka's earnest intensity combined with archaic language choice is perfectly in character

jokes.text: A big nose and a Green Card.
jokes.setup: Latka asks what Alex has that he doesn't
jokes.punchline: Latka lists his own immigrant disadvantages matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Latka's innocent honesty about perceived disadvantages is both touching and funny

jokes.text: I don't allow no family get-togethers in my garage. Well, I just want to talk to him for two minutes. Give me a buck.
jokes.setup: Louie refuses to allow family visits
jokes.punchline: He immediately sells out his principle for a dollar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie - petty rules instantly abandoned for tiny profit

jokes.text: One was good-looking, one was smart, and one was you.
jokes.setup: Louie asks how Monica knows who he is
jokes.punchline: Monica's description leaves Louie as neither good-looking nor smart
jokes.explanation: Beautifully structured insult that lets the audience fill in the blank

jokes.text: Well, meow.
jokes.setup: Monica delivers a subtle but cutting insult
jokes.punchline: Louie's response suggests he's impressed despite being insulted
jokes.explanation: Louie's grudging respect for being insulted well shows his appreciation for wit

jokes.text: Oh, I'm in luck... a human being.
jokes.setup: Monica has just met Louie
jokes.punchline: She's relieved to find someone who qualifies as human
jokes.explanation: Sets up Bobby as the contrast to Louie while establishing Monica's wit

jokes.text: Well, do you know that you and I are forbidden to love? It's a shame, it's a big shame. Because we would have made beautiful babies together.
jokes.setup: Bobby meets Tony's sister for the first time
jokes.punchline: Bobby immediately declares their love forbidden and discusses hypothetical babies
jokes.explanation: Bobby's immediate romantic delusions are in character but the execution is a bit overwrought

jokes.text: I never thought I'd say this, but don't go away.
jokes.setup: Bobby has just delivered his awkward romantic speech
jokes.punchline: Monica finds Bobby preferable to whoever is coming next
jokes.explanation: Monica's dry response suggests she's dreading meeting someone even worse than Bobby

jokes.text: I was born there.
jokes.setup: Long pause after Jim asks if Monica lived in Spokane
jokes.punchline: Jim's delayed realization delivered as if it's profound
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - the long pause and delayed obvious realization captures his spaced-out character

jokes.text: It's been a long time since I've seen a dress from Spokane.
jokes.setup: Jim is trying to make conversation about Monica's dress
jokes.punchline: Jim acts as if dresses have regional characteristics
jokes.explanation: Jim's innocent absurdity - treating clothing geography as real knowledge

jokes.text: Smooth, Iggy. Real smooth.
jokes.setup: Jim delivers his awkward Spokane dress line
jokes.punchline: Alex sarcastically comments on Jim's 'smooth' approach
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic reaction, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Except for the gong. You mean the gonnnnng? Yeah. You know it.
jokes.setup: Jim says the flute is his favorite instrument except for the gong
jokes.punchline: Monica makes the gong sound and Jim acts like she's demonstrated deep knowledge
jokes.explanation: Jim's earnest appreciation for Monica's gong impression shows his sweet simplicity

jokes.text: [Extended Vivaldi humming duet between Jim and Monica]
jokes.setup: Jim and Monica start humming Vivaldi together
jokes.punchline: They create an elaborate musical moment in a taxi garage
jokes.explanation: Extended visual/audio gag that establishes their connection through shared absurdity

jokes.text: No Vivaldi in the garage.
jokes.setup: Jim and Monica have been humming classical music
jokes.punchline: Louie creates a specific rule against Vivaldi in his garage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie line - treating high culture as just another workplace disruption to regulate

jokes.text: One of these days I got to get it together and go to the symphony. I'm pretty sure New York has one.
jokes.setup: Jim and Monica have been discussing classical music
jokes.punchline: Jim is uncertain whether New York has a symphony orchestra
jokes.explanation: Jim's uncertainty about basic NYC cultural institutions perfectly captures his detachment

jokes.text: Well, you don't have to tag along. Maybe we could go together. Huh... I just got a rush.
jokes.setup: Monica suggests they could go to the symphony together instead of Jim tagging along
jokes.punchline: Jim's immediate physical/emotional reaction to being asked on a date
jokes.explanation: Jim's innocent excitement about being asked on a date is both funny and touching

jokes.text: In fact, at one point Alex said, 'I can't get a pause in edgewise.'
jokes.setup: Monica is telling Tony about her lunch with Alex
jokes.punchline: She misquotes the phrase 'word in edgewise' as 'pause in edgewise'
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on a common phrase that reveals Monica talks too much while creating new humor

jokes.text: Ah! What an icebreaker.
jokes.setup: Monica reveals Alex complained about not being able to get a word in
jokes.punchline: Tony sarcastically calls Alex's complaint an 'icebreaker'
jokes.explanation: Tony's sarcasm about Alex's dating skills, though the line itself is fairly standard

jokes.text: Your husband was your type. An Electrolux salesman with every allergy in the book who painted turtles for relaxation. You know, you always make so much of that one time he painted the turtle.
jokes.setup: Tony criticizes Monica's taste in men
jokes.punchline: Monica's defensive response suggests turtle-painting was a regular hobby, not one incident
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that gets funnier when Monica's defense reveals it happened multiple times

jokes.text: We all got our faults. I'm messy around the house. You fall for garbage.
jokes.setup: Tony admits they both have faults and lists his
jokes.punchline: Tony's assessment of Monica's fault is brutally direct
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tony honesty - equating being messy with having terrible taste in men

jokes.text: The last girl Jim went out with lived in a forest and ate bark.
jokes.setup: Monica says she's interested in Jim
jokes.punchline: Tony describes Jim's previous girlfriend as essentially feral
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character detail that's both absurd and believable for Jim's dating history

jokes.text: No, it doesn't have anything to do with wooden shoes.
jokes.setup: Monica is on phone with Jim discussing going Dutch for dinner
jokes.punchline: Jim apparently thought 'going Dutch' literally involved wooden shoes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim confusion - taking idioms literally in the most innocent way

jokes.text: Is this the bill already?
jokes.setup: Waiter gives Jim a note from Monica
jokes.punchline: Jim assumes any paper from restaurant staff must be the check
jokes.explanation: Jim's confusion about restaurant protocols is in character but not exceptional

jokes.text: We can't have dinner together. Why not? I don't know. You didn't go into it.
jokes.setup: Jim reads Monica's note saying she couldn't wait
jokes.punchline: Jim treats the brief note as if it's a complete rejection without questioning
jokes.explanation: Jim's literal interpretation and passive acceptance perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: My face grows hair very fast. I shave, and it seems like only a matter of days before it's back.
jokes.setup: Jim apologizes for forgetting to shave
jokes.punchline: Jim describes normal beard growth as if it's remarkably fast
jokes.explanation: Jim's innocent amazement at normal biological processes is perfectly in character

jokes.text: I don't have much hair on my body at all. Compared to my face, my skin is like a smooth baby pumpkin.
jokes.setup: Jim is trying to make conversation on their date
jokes.punchline: Jim describes his body hair situation using an unusual food comparison
jokes.explanation: Jim's inappropriate oversharing combined with bizarre imagery creates perfect cringe comedy

jokes.text: I didn't mean to talk erotic.
jokes.setup: Jim has just described his body hair situation
jokes.punchline: Jim's innocent apology for accidentally being 'erotic'
jokes.explanation: Jim's complete misunderstanding of what constitutes erotic talk is both innocent and hilarious

jokes.text: No. I just learned what I should order. Took me all day.
jokes.setup: Monica is impressed by Jim's French
jokes.punchline: Jim reveals he memorized specific phrases just for this dinner
jokes.explanation: Sweet revelation that Jim put enormous effort into impressing Monica, perfectly in character

jokes.text: The hard part was phoning all over town trying to find a restaurant that serves that.
jokes.setup: Jim reveals he memorized French phrases for dinner
jokes.punchline: Jim went even further, finding a restaurant that serves those specific dishes
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing the full extent of Jim's romantic effort and planning

jokes.text: You're turning my head.
jokes.setup: Jim has revealed his elaborate romantic preparation
jokes.punchline: Monica admits she's genuinely charmed by Jim's efforts
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment that shows Monica genuinely falling for Jim's sincere efforts

jokes.text: When I think of me, I smile, too. We're both thinking of me.
jokes.setup: Monica says Jim makes her happy and she thinks of him and smiles
jokes.punchline: Jim points out they're both thinking of him, as if this is a remarkable coincidence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim logic - treating a romantic moment as an interesting statistical observation

jokes.text: Thanks, Tony. I do feel a little better.
jokes.setup: Tony offers to let Jim punch him for interfering with his relationship
jokes.punchline: Jim sincerely thanks Tony as if the offer itself was therapeutic
jokes.explanation: Jim's innocent appreciation for Tony's gesture, missing the point entirely

jokes.text: You don't think enough of me to go to bed with my sister.
jokes.setup: Tony is angry at Alex for not pursuing Monica
jokes.punchline: Tony frames Alex's restraint as an insult to Tony himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twisted logic that perfectly captures Tony's wounded pride and misplaced priorities

jokes.text: Of course, we have a lot of room here. We'll need a bathroom schedule.
jokes.setup: Jim says he sleeps over, Tony threatens to stay until Jim leaves
jokes.punchline: Monica practically discusses logistics for their standoff
jokes.explanation: Monica's dry practicality in the face of Tony's dramatic behavior

jokes.text: It's Chicago '68 all over again.
jokes.setup: Tony is physically carrying Jim around
jokes.punchline: Jim compares this personal conflict to historic civil unrest
jokes.explanation: Jim's random historical reference to describe being manhandled is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Well, my shorts are riding a little high. Otherwise...
jokes.setup: Tony asks if Jim is alright after being carried around
jokes.punchline: Jim's only complaint is a minor clothing adjustment
jokes.explanation: Jim's matter-of-fact response to physical comedy, focusing on minor discomfort

jokes.text: We're on threads of hope, trying to weave us a basket.
jokes.setup: Tony asks if Jim and Monica are 'on anything' (drugs)
jokes.punchline: Jim gives a poetic non-answer that sounds profound but means nothing
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim - profound-sounding nonsense that perfectly captures his character's mystical bent

jokes.text: No, I bat about .500. That's all right, honey. I don't get 'em all, either.
jokes.setup: Tony asks if Monica understood Jim's cryptic statement
jokes.punchline: Tony admits he only understands half of what Jim says, Monica agrees
jokes.explanation: Self-aware acknowledgment of Jim's incomprehensible dialogue, treating it like a batting average

jokes.text: And I think I've learned the most important thing of all: I love being lifted.
jokes.setup: Jim is supposed to share what he learned from the experience
jokes.punchline: Jim's takeaway is that he enjoyed being physically carried around
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim ending - missing the entire emotional point to focus on physical sensation
